title: ARTEMIS: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Economically Constrained Market Dynamics

update_history.reason: Stage 4 code generation + end-to-end validation completed. Repo built (40 files: 6 ARTEMIS modules, 5 baselines, DSLOB generator, 5 CLI entrypoints). Validated via placeholder-seed DSLOB pipeline (real seed data unavailable). 1 OOM bug found and fixed in scripts/evaluate.py and scripts/plot_figures.py (unbatched full-dataset forward pass). 1 additional paper-internal inconsistency noticed (Time-IMM DirAcc: 96.0% in abstract vs 86.0% in Table 2) and documented in README for Stage 6 to formally catalogue.

Stage 5 notebook generation completed: primary reproduce notebook (21 cells) and exploratory notebook (13 cells) both generated and executed end-to-end via nbclient with zero errors after fixing 2 issues found during validation (a stray-escaped-quote syntax bug in the notebook build script; a d_z/d_w dimensional mismatch in the Component 3 physics-losses demo). Surfaced a Medium-severity finding for Stage 6: configs/config.yaml's default d_z=64/d_w=16 combo silently zeroes the market-price-of-risk loss for all real training runs using the shipped defaults.

Stage 6 results comparison completed. No user experimental results were submitted; DSLOB is structurally unreproducible (unnamed seed dataset per Section 3.4). No numeric reproducibility score computed (requires_manual_review: true, score: null). Full hallucination analysis performed: 3 structural findings (1 High -- DSLOB unreproducibility, a paper limitation; 1 Medium-High -- the d_z/d_w config bug, escalated after direct code verification; 1 Low, already fixed), 9 parametric findings (all pre-flagged ASSUMED hyperparameters), 0 omissions, plus 4 paper-internal inconsistencies formally catalogued.

affiliations: Department of Electronics and Electrical Engineering, BITS Pilani, Hyderabad Campus
extended_provenance.human_review_reason: SIR confidence 0.64 (below 0.65 threshold); two paper-internal inconsistencies (XGBoost baseline claimed but never reported; DSLOB feature count discrepancy 59 vs 85); core architecture hyperparameters never numerically specified (latent dim, SDE step size, loss weights); paper's own Appendix A.4.1 self-flags the Feynman-Kac PDE regularizer as theoretically incomplete.
